Author: von Saldern, Jakob G.R./Schmidt, Oliver T./Godbersen, Philipp/Reumschüssel, J. Moritz/Colonius, Tim
Title: Band-Ensemble Spectral Proper Orthogonal Decomposition with Frequency Attribution
Abstract: This study presents band-ensemble Spectral Proper Orthogonal Decomposition (bSPOD). The approach is inspired by frequency smoothing, a method used to reduce estimator variance in power spectral density estimates, and is here extended to SPOD. The algorithm estimates SPOD modes from consecutive Fourier coefficients obtained from a single Fourier transform of the full time record and thus avoids time segmentation. In this study, bSPOD is applied to artificial test data and to a PIV data set of a broadband-tonal cavity flow. Compared to the more commonly used Welch-based SPOD formulation, bSPOD reduces spectral leakage, permits increased frequency resolution, and retains frequency information of tonal components at comparable computational cost. These features enable reduced estimator variance while maintaining low bias for tonal components, making bSPOD particularly effective for broadband-tonal flows

Author: Nakkanti, Akhil/Wadas, Michael/Colonius, Tim
Title: Azimuthal vortex instability in turbulent jets
Abstract: An axisymmetrically forced initially-laminar jet at a Reynolds number is investigated to identify the presence of Widnall instability.
Publisher: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics

Author: Sleeman, Michael Kenneth Elliott
Title: Nonlinear Disturbance Evolution in Boundary Layers Using the One-Way Navier-Stokes Equations
Abstract: Laminar-turbulent transition prediction for boundary-layer flows is a pacing item in engineering design.

Author: Lee, Marcus Kuok Kuan/Colonius, Tim/Mckeon, Beverley J.
Title: Instabilities in the Flow Over a Spinning Disk at Angle of Attack
Abstract: Three-dimensional laminar flow over an inclined spinning disk is investigated at a Reynolds number of Re=500 and an angle of attack of α=25°, for tip-speed ratios up to 3.

Author: Padovan, Alberto/Anantharaman, Vishal/Rowlley, Clarence W./Vollmer, Blaine/Colonius, Tim/Bodony, Daniel J.
Title: Resolvent4py: A parallel Python package for analysis model reduction and control of large-scale linear systems
Abstract: In this paper, we present resolvent4py, a parallel Python package for the analysis, model reduction and control of large-scale linear systems with millions or billions of degrees of freedom.

Author: Arun, Rahul
Title: Beyond Symmetry: Normality-Based Analysis of Velocity Gradients in Turbulent Flows
Abstract: Small-scale turbulence is a hallmark of countless natural and engineered flows.

Author: Sleeman, Michael K/Colonius, Tim
Title: Greedy recursion parameter selection for the One-Way Navier-Stokes (OWNS) equations
Abstract: The One-Way Navier-Stokes (OWNS) equations use recursive filtering to construct efficient, well-posed one-way approximations to linear hyperbolic systems.
